methods.rationale: Applies typical Series C → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.7x) to $70M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 5 same-sector + same-stage peers in Space & Aerospace. Range: $1.0B - $8.0B.

data.narrative:
Fleet Space Technologies, a Series C-stage space and aerospace company that has raised $70M+, was valued using two applicable triangulation methods against a peer set of 5 private companies in the same sector and stage (Sierra Space, Varda Space Industries, Skyryse, Isar Aerospace, Loft Orbital) and 5 public comparables (RKLB, PL, LUNR, ASTS, SATL).

Stage-progression analysis, applying a 1.7x step-up multiplier to the $70M raised baseline, implies a valuation of $119M. The peer-median method, across the five same-sector, same-stage private companies, yields $1.1B, with individual peer valuations ranging from $1.0B to $8.0B. Public-comp anchoring was not applied due to the absence of disclosed revenue figures. The wide dispersion in peer valuations—particularly Sierra Space at $8.0B versus the median of $1.1B—reflects heterogeneity in market positioning or funding dynamics within frontier space.

The two applicable methods triangulate to a range of $101M (low) to $1.3B (high), with a median of $634M. This wide span reflects material uncertainty around whether Fleet Space should be valued closer to its historical funding-round progression or to the upper-quartile peer set. Key drivers of valuation within this range include competitive differentiation, revenue traction or nearness to monetization, and relative claims on the addressable market. Further narrowing would require disclosure of revenue, customer concentration, or unit economics.
